items.body: The Charles Schwab Challenge returns to Colonial Country Club in Fort Worth as one of the Tour’s most respected stops, with Ben Griffin back to defend his title. Ludvig Aberg and Justin Thomas headline a strong field, and Colonial’s tight, traditional setup should reward precision, patience, and smart ball striking more than raw power. It’s the kind of week where a steady game can beat a flashy one if the putter cooperates.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1000 to +1200):
items.body: One of the betting favorites and a perfect fit for a course that rewards elite ball striking and controlled iron play.

items.label: Justin Thomas (+1200 to +1400):
items.body: A strong contender at Colonial thanks to his creativity, course management, and ability to get hot with the putter.

items.label: Ben Griffin (+1800 to +2500):
items.body: The defending champion and a live option if last year’s comfort level at Colonial carries over.

items.label: Russell Henley (+1800 to +2500):
items.body: A steady, precision-based player whose accuracy and iron game are tailor-made for Colonial.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2200 to +3000):
items.body: An elite approach player whose consistency can keep him in the mix on a demanding par-70.

items.label: Wyndham Clark (+2500 to +3500):
items.body: A power player who can still contend if he balances aggression with control off the tee.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2500 to +3500):
items.body: A polished all-around player whose shot-making and patience suit Colonial well.

items.label: Keegan Bradley (+3000 to +4000):
items.body: A grinder with a dependable tee-to-green profile who can hang around on a tough setup.

items.label: Rickie Fowler (+3000 to +4000):
items.body: A dangerous mid-range option if his irons and short game click for four rounds.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Colonial Country Club in Fort Worth, Texas.

items.label: Established:
items.body: 1946, making it the longest-running PGA Tour event hosted at the same venue.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Ben Griffin won the 2025 edition.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Colonial remains one of the Tour’s most respected invitational events and a longstanding Memorial Day weekend staple.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Colonial Country Club, Fort Worth, Texas.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: May 28 through May 31, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $9.9 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 132-player invitational, 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A tight 7,289-yard par-70 that rewards shot shaping, accuracy, and disciplined positioning.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: PGA Tour broadcast and streaming coverage is expected throughout the week.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs May 28 through May 31, 2026.
